title: Avalon House
description: Avalon House is a coastal residence shaped by its bushland setting on Sydney’s Northern Beaches. The project draws from its immediate context, using a restrained palette and careful spatial planning to create a calm and grounded living environment. The interior is defined by clarity and consistency.

title: Martis Camp
location: 7951 Fleur Du Lac, Truckee, CA 96161, USA
description: After working together on their primary home in San Francisco, this family of four returned to Dumican Mosey Architects (DMA) for a new project: a sophisticated yet liveable retreat in the famed Martis Camp community north of Lake Tahoe. Surrounded by natural beauty, Martis Camp encourages architecture that is contextually sensitive without prescribing a definitive style. In response to these conditions DMA designed a contemporary two-story home that is thoughtfully integrated into the site with an interior experience that immerses occupants in the landscape.
facts: 7951 Fleur Du Lac, Truckee, CA 96161, USA

title: House as a Garden
description: This project transforms an early 20th-century warehouse in Red Hook, Brooklyn into a light-filled, highly adaptable urban residence. What began as a raw industrial storage space, vast, uninsulated, and undefined, became an opportunity to reimagine domestic life within an expansive volume. Working closely with a first-time homeowner, the design process was deeply collaborative and exploratory.

title: Amayadori House
description: Amayadori House redefines the interior wall as a light-transmitting element rather than a static divider, addressing the challenge of a deep, single-aspect apartment with limited daylight. Instead of adopting a fully open-plan layout or relying on artificial lighting, the design maintains distinct zones while inserting carefully positioned glass apertures within custom timber partitions. Acting as internal clerestories, these openings redistribute natural light laterally, allowing it to penetrate deeper into the space while preserving privacy, acoustic separation, and intimacy—key for a home that also functions as a discreet speakeasy.

title: Mangata
description: Set along the shores of Taihu Lake—a landscape long associated with the literati ideal of reclusive living, the project orchestrates a quiet interplay of nature, light, and materiality. Boundaries between inside and out dissolve as the courtyard, conservatory, and quiet corners flow into one another, shaping a fluid rhythm of everyday life. Above the lake is the calm of daily rituals; beneath it, a hidden tide of inner richness.

title: ParkLife & the Village
category: FRAME Awards 2023, Co-Living Complex of the Year
description: ParkLife rethinks apartment living as a social and environmental system, combining communal gardens, passive design, and generous shared amenities within a dense urban housing model.
facts: FRAME Awards 2023, Co-Living Complex of the Year

title: Can Santacilia
description: Can Santacilia restores a layered historic palace into 15 dwellings and shared spaces, balancing archaeological sensitivity with contemporary residential calm.

title: Arkadia
category: FRAME Awards 2021, Co-Living Complex of the Year
description: Arkadia brings together recycled brick character, planted shared spaces, and socially minded apartment planning to challenge conventional urban housing models.
facts: FRAME Awards 2021, Co-Living Complex of the Year

title: Marylebone Apartment
category: FRAME Awards 2020, Small Apartment of the Year
description: Marylebone Apartment uses crafted joinery and careful spatial compression to make a compact home feel richly layered, calm and highly efficient.
facts: FRAME Awards 2020, Small Apartment of the Year

title: Casa Burés
category: FRAME Awards 2020, Co-Living Complex of the Year
description: Casa Burés transforms a modernist landmark into a richly restored shared living environment, where decorative heritage is paired with residential hospitality and contemporary comfort.
facts: FRAME Awards 2020, Co-Living Complex of the Year

title: Oosterwold Co-living Complex
category: FRAME Awards 2019, Co-Living Complex of the Year
description: FRAME-recognized residential project documented on ArchDaily.
facts: FRAME Awards 2019, Co-Living Complex of the Year
Referenced via ArchDaily and FRAME recognition

title: House AQ
description: For large-scale residential projects, the primary task is to construct a suitable scene and scale, based on a shared understanding of life with the client. Even with the ample square footage, Designer Ruoqiao Chai maximized the utility of every space rather than creating empty rooms that might only be used once or twice a year. So, we don’t have any amenities such as guest room, home theatre, recreation room, or gym, we focused on better serving practical living needs.

title: Fletcher Street House
description: Fletcher Street is a three-level residence set on a constrained coastal site overlooking Bondi Beach. The project responds to limited access to light and space by turning constraint into a defining architectural move. The interior is organised around a central sculptural staircase, which operates as both circulation and a vertical light well.

title: 27B Aida Street
description: “A home that brings light in, while keeping the heat out.” Located within Singapore’s dense urban fabric, Aida Street explores how an ageing inter-terrace house can be transformed through environmental responsiveness, spatial clarity, and long-term adaptability. Owned by the Chua family for over 30 years, the original house was affected by leaks, poor ventilation, and dark internal spaces caused by its deep plan and challenging east–west orientation. Rather than demolishing the house, the family chose to retain and transform it, preserving both its structure and emotional significance.

title: DJ's House
description: The project redefines domestic space by merging family life with a performative environment for music. Designed for a pair of DJ performers and music producers, the house integrates two custom DJ stations, indoors and outdoors, embedding sound, rhythm, and hosting into the spatial fabric of everyday life. Innovation lies in the transformation of the home into a live cultural infrastructure.

title: Designer Wu Bin‘s Anji Retreat
location: Tianhuangpingzhen, Anji County, Huzhou, Zhejiang, China, 313302
description: In the silent rhythm of Anji's distant mountains, Wu Bin draws from China's reclusive literati tradition—where home is not ownership of nature, but belonging to it like a tree, rooted in earth and stretching toward the sky. Tucked within a valley, the small cabin steps back, yielding to mountain silence, wind's path, and light through trees—becoming part of the scenery. Through "formlessness" and "subtlety," the dwelling defers to the land, restoring space as a vessel for the spirit.
facts: Tianhuangpingzhen, Anji County, Huzhou, Zhejiang, China, 313302

title: 2 Allamanda Grove
description: Located within a prime residential neighbourhood in land-scarce Singapore, Screen House explores how architecture can reconcile privacy, environmental performance, and contemporary family living within a dense urban context. Designed for a young family of four, the project adopts a layered architectural approach that balances openness and seclusion while responding sensitively to climate and lifestyle. At the core of the design is a rethinking of privacy—not as complete enclosure, but as a calibrated mediation between exposure and openness.

title: Tonganli
description: This project reinterprets a traditional Shikumen-style villa through a contemporary spatial language that bridges heritage and modern living. Rather than treating the villa’s small scale as a limitation, the design embraces it as an opportunity to cultivate intimacy, flexibility and emotional depth. Innovation lies in redefining how space operates within constraints.

title: Harper House
location: Gramercy Park, New York, NY, USA
description: The restoration of Harper House, the 1844 Greek Revival on Gramercy Park, centered on returning a historically fragmented structure, back to a coherent single-family residence while honoring the layered narrative uncovered during renovation. Innovation: The innovation lies in treating the interiors as an archaeological and design continuum rather than a fixed historic artifact. The brief, to create a family home that could also host musical performances and philanthropic events, pushed beyond conventional restoration.
facts: Gramercy Park, New York, NY, USA

title: W Residence Penthouse Dubai a Study in Restraint
location: West - Plm Jumeirah - Crescent Rd - The Palm Jumeirah - Dubai - United Arab Emirates
description: The project is conceived as a spatial interpretation of calm within a context often defined by excess. Located in the W Residences, Palm Jumeirah, the apartment explores how restraint, proportion and material continuity can generate a sense of quiet clarity without relying on gesture. The project includes a private lift lobby that serves as the entrance to the penthouse, establishing the architectural language from the moment of arrival.
facts: West - Plm Jumeirah - Crescent Rd - The Palm Jumeirah - Dubai - United Arab Emirates
original_title: W Residence Penthouse Dubai a Study in Restraint

title: La maison de la Baie de l'Ours
description: Architecture is not merely a shelter; it is an invitation to dialogue between humans and nature. When its boundaries dissolve, it becomes an experience—a sensation, an inhabited landscape. La Maison de la Baie de l’Ours draws its strength from the striking beauty of its site: its relationship to the horizon, the lake, and the rocky headlands.
original_title: La maison de la Baie de l'Ours

title: Porto House
location: Fazenda Boa Vista, Serra Azul - SP, 14230-000, Brazil
description: Located within the landscape of a scenic golf course, Residence Porto was conceived to establish a harmonious relationship between architecture and nature. Positioned slightly above the access street, the residence takes advantage of its subtle elevation to expand views across the surrounding greenery, creating a sense of openness despite the flat terrain. Its L-shaped layout organizes the program around outdoor living spaces, strengthening the integration between interior environments and the landscape while encouraging natural light and cross ventilation throughout the house.
facts: Fazenda Boa Vista, Serra Azul - SP, 14230-000, Brazil

title: The Pretty House
description: The Pretty House explores how colour and geometry can operate as architectural devices rather than decorative elements. Conceived as a study in spatial composition, the project reimagines the domestic interior as a controlled graphic framework where form, mass and negative space are carefully orchestrated. At the core of the design is a checkerboard floor that functions as an organising system.

title: Collector’s Home
description: This project presents an innovative rethinking of multigenerational living by dissolving conventional spatial boundaries and introducing a fluid, centralised living structure. By merging two apartments into a cohesive environment, the design establishes a new residential paradigm where openness and privacy coexist through a carefully orchestrated spatial hierarchy. Creatively, the project reinterprets Eastern aesthetics within a contemporary framework.

title: Snider House
description: Snider House (c.1828) offers an inspiring example of how a heritage protected home can be both preserved and transformed, balancing respect for history with a vision for contemporary living. Innovation: The task of renovating this significant home required an innovative, deeply contextual approach to allow for an architectural dialogue between past and present. The home is an amalgamation of experiences, where the addition of new pieces, sections, iterations, and materials embody a rich layering of ideas.

title: Tekoá House
location: Rodovia Miguel Melhado Campos, km83,5,s/n - Aglomeração Urbana de Jundiaí, Itupeva - SP, 13295-000, Brazil
description: Tekoá Residence emerges from a sensitive reading of the landscape, translating the site’s topography into an architecture that balances lightness, openness, and permanence. Set on a sloped terrain, the house is organized through an L-shaped configuration that naturally adapts to the land while creating protected outdoor spaces and strengthening the dialogue between architecture and nature. The composition of solids and voids generates a dynamic volumetry, combining sophistication with a welcoming atmosphere shaped by natural materials, generous spaces, and lush landscaping.
facts: Rodovia Miguel Melhado Campos, km83,5,s/n - Aglomeração Urbana de Jundiaí, Itupeva - SP, 13295-000, Brazil

title: Villa Boira
location: Parcelación Villas de la Candelaria, Envigado, Antioquia, Colombia
description: Located in the mountains of Envigado, Colombia, Villa Boira transforms an existing structure into a permanent home. Rather than demolishing, the project works through processes of conscious reuse and adaptation with minimal environmental impact. What already exists is understood as active matter: preserving what is essential while reinterpreting what holds value.
facts: Parcelación Villas de la Candelaria, Envigado, Antioquia, Colombia

title: Praia Brava House
description: Between native vegetation and the ocean, Praia Brava House is precisely positioned on a steeply sloped site within a rare enclave of continuous Atlantic Forest in Itajaí, Santa Catarina, Brazil, immediately beside Balneário Camboriú, one of the densest coastlines in the world. Against the vertical excess of its neighbour, this project is a horizontal counter-argument: a house that preserves and displaces as little as possible, its shape determined by the existing trees mapped on site and treated as fixed. The stepped volumes, the recesses, the asymmetrical plan: each is a consequence of a tree preserved, a root system respected, a canopy left intact.

title: Author
description: This project sets new standards for residential design by seamlessly integrating functionality, comfort, and luxury. The open-concept design balances social spaces and private retreats, using high-end materials like Brasilia exotic stone and Tencel to create an atmosphere that feels both contemporary and timeless. The space is not only a visual experience but also a sensory one, with details like handcrafted brass lamps and the tactility of natural wood surfaces.

title: Ibaté Apartment
location: Av. Sabiá, 26 - Moema, São Paulo - SP, 04515-000, Brazil
description: Designed for a young couple preparing for the arrival of their first child, the Ibaté Apartment was conceived as a contemporary living environment where architecture, atmosphere, and technology operate as a unified system. The residence explores a refined spatial language inspired by Italian interiors while responding to the clients’ desire for a home that balances hosting with the intimacy of family life. Rather than radically altering the apartment delivered by the developer, the project focused on precise interventions that redefine how the space is experienced, prioritising continuity, warmth, and flexibility over formal excess.
facts: Av. Sabiá, 26 - Moema, São Paulo - SP, 04515-000, Brazil

title: AMS House
location: Porto Feliz, State of São Paulo, 18540-000, Brazil
description: AMS House's design strategy centers on restraint, climatic intelligence, and the creation of a calm domestic atmosphere. Rather than treating the house as an isolated object, the project organizes itself through fragmented volumes, covered transitions, and gradual sensory shifts that respond directly to the sloped terrain and surrounding forest. Innovation: The project reinterprets vernacular Brazilian construction techniques through a contemporary spatial language.
facts: Porto Feliz, State of São Paulo, 18540-000, Brazil
